beats.say: Let's watch our arrow fly in real time. The split screen merges into a single view as the arrow begins its journey through the air.
Notice how the horizontal velocity stays perfectly constant at 34.6 m/s throughout the entire flight. No forces act horizontally, so this component never changes.
Meanwhile, gravity steadily reduces the vertical velocity from its initial 20 m/s. Watch how it slows down, stops, then accelerates downward.
The velocity vector tells the whole story — it rotates and shrinks vertically while staying constant horizontally. This combination creates the curved path we see.
